rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Validate that binary expressions in guarantees use boolean types on both sides of '=>'.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code that are not present in the AADL model or its dependencies.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are declared or visible in the current scope.
Validate that expression types match expected AGREE types (bool, int, real) to prevent '<error>' type issues.
Prevent invalid or missing helper declarations that cause 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in constant and equation declarations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports should be in component type scopes.
Implementation-level bindings should be in component implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections.
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce identifiers or types that are not declared or visible in the current AADL scope.
Do not write binary expressions in guarantees where one side is not of type 'bool'.
Do not reference package-qualified identifiers not present in the model or its configured dependencies.
